sourceMovie.title: Four Brothers
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Four Brothers for the same reason it broke through in 2005: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. John Singleton keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Gone in Sixty Seconds
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Four Brothers clicked for you, Gone in Sixty Seconds (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Four Brothers?
faq.answer: Try Gone in Sixty Seconds, Run All Night, The Chaser—each shares DNA with Four Brothers while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Four Brothers?
faq.answer: Four Brothers (2005) is best described as Action, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Four Brothers worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
